The '''''Bivalva contemplativa''''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Clam/Legends|clams]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Perilix]]<ref name="Yoda" /> of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies Region]].<ref name="Atlas">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Perilix system/Legends|Perilix system]]</ref> They exhibited multigenerational narrative semaphores, which caused dispute among the [[xeno-ethnologist]]s of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] as to whether the ''Bivalva contemplativa'' possessed true [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]]. Some of the ethnologists argued this characteristic only reflected a form of [[Hive mind/Legends|hive building]] insufficient to demonstrate true sentience, while others argued it constituted conversation between individuals. Despite this argument remaining unresolved up to [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], the controversy earned them the nickname of the "'''thinking clams of Perilix'''."<ref name="Yoda" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Bivalva contemplativa'' were created by [[Sean Stewart]] for his novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', published by [[Del Rey]] in [[2004]]. They later received a mention in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', although it referred to them as the "Bivalva contemplative."<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|20|Perilix}}</ref> In reality, Bivalvia is a class used in biological classification for a type of [[Mollusk/Legends|mollusk]] species that has two shells, as opposed to classes such as [[Gastropod/Legends|gastropods]] and [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopods]], which only have one shell.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://paleo.cortland.edu/tutorial/Bivalves/bivalvia.htm|text=Bivalvia|archivedate=20180130103234}}</ref>
